ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., sensor, actuator, controller) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model number of the device */,
  "device_serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the device */,
  "device_manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "device_location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  "device_status" TEXT /* Status of the device (e.g., online, offline) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "registers" (
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the register belongs to */,
  "register_address" INTEGER /* Register address */,
  "parameter_notation" TEXT /* Parameter notation (e.g., O2FT, CYC2) */,
  "parameter_description" TEXT /* Parameter description */,
  "register_type" TEXT /* Type of register (e.g., input, output) */,
  "register_size" TEXT /* Size of the register (e.g., 16-bit, 32-bit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_registers_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register the parameter belongs to */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "range_low" REAL /* Low value of the parameter range */,
  "range_high" REAL /* High value of the parameter range */,
  "default_value" REAL /* Default value of the parameter */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., R/W, R/O) */,
  "parameter_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the parameter (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit) */,
  "parameter_tolerance" REAL /* Tolerance of the parameter (e.g., ±1%, ±2%) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role (e.g., administrator, engineer, operator) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_control" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Access type (e.g., temporary, permanent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trails" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit trail entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date the change was made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, delete) */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the parameter */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the parameter */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "real_time_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the parameter */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data point */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., good, bad) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_real_time_data_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_real_time_data_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_real_time_data_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm event */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of alarm (e.g., high-high, low-low) */,
  "alarm_date" TEXT /* Date the alarm occurred */,
  "alarm_value" REAL /* Value of the parameter that triggered the alarm */,
  "alarm_status" TEXT /* Status of the alarm (e.g., active, cleared) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., startup, shutdown) */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date the event occurred */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "event_status" TEXT /* Status of the event (e.g., completed, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms_acknowledgment" (
  "ack_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm acknowledgment */,
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alarm */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who acknowledged the alarm */,
  "ack_date" TEXT /* Date the alarm was acknowledged */,
  "ack_notes" TEXT /* Notes related to the alarm acknowledgment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ack_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_acknowledgment_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_acknowledgment_alarm_id FOREIGN KEY ("alarm_id") REFERENCES alarms ("alarm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_response" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event response */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who responded to the event */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the event was responded to */,
  "response_notes" TEXT /* Notes related to the event response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_response_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_response_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_config" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration item */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration item */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration item */,
  "config_type" TEXT /* Type of the configuration item (e.g., IP address, port number) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)
